transcript_facts:
Here's a list of atomic claims extracted from the transcript, adhering to the specified guidelines:

*   The patient reports fatigue for the past two months.
*   The patient denies blood in stool.
*   The clinician suspects cervical radiculopathy.
*   The clinician plans a cervical MRI.
*   The patient injured his wrist while walking up and down the stairs doing laundry.
*   The patient slipped and tried to catch himself putting his arms out.
*   The patient’s wrist started to hurt real bad and it got real swollen.
*   The patient’s pain is like a nine on a scale of one to ten.
*   The patient has been doing the ice thing and uses an ace wrap.
*   The patient has taken ibuprofen, which helps a little.
*   The clinician is trying to think how long ago this injury happened.
*   This injury happened yesterday morning.
*   The patient bumped his wrist.
*   The patient’s pain does not extend very far.
*   The patient’s pain is primarily along his wrist.
*   The patient feels some tingling.
*   The patient is unsure if the tingling is just due to swelling.
*   The clinician is going to do a physical exam.
*   The clinician palpates the wrist and finds tenderness.
*   The clinician observes normal range of movement with the right wrist.
*   The clinician finds positive abduction adduction pain on movement.
*   The patient is able to make a fist, but not tightly.
*   The clinician examines the fingers, metacarpals intact.
*   The clinician observes obvious swelling and bruising.
*   The patient reports that the pain goes away when he takes ibuprofen.
*   The patient feels the pain most of the time.
*   The patient’s pain is worse when his arm is raised and elevated.
*   The patient feels better when his arm is kept straight.
*   The clinician notes elevated blood pressure, likely due to pain.
*   The patient’s heart rate is elevated at approximately eighty beats per minute.
*   The patient’s respiratory rate is normal at twenty breaths per minute.
*   The patient has a positive colles’ fracture.
*   The patient will receive a thumb spica brace for today.
*   The clinician will prescribe fifty milligrams of ultram every six hours.
*   The clinician will prescribe physical therapy a few weeks after surgery.
*   The surgery will be performed as quickly as possible.
*   The patient will stay overnight after surgery.
*   The patient will wear the brace for approximately six weeks.
*   The patient will attend physical therapy three times a week.
*   The patient’s body will become stronger after the fracture.
*   The fracture appears extra-articular and proximal to the radial ulnar joint.
*   Dorsal angulation of the distal fracture fragment is present.
*   Ulnar styloid fracture is present.
